## Data Validity

- Source completeness semantics: for S2, an incident_id present in S1 but absent from S2 means "not yet closed as of the 2026-06-22T23:59Z extract" — not "event absent" and not export corruption — this reading is supported by two pieces of evidence: (a) every missing row's `opened_at` is well inside the collection window (no missing rows from incidents that would already have a `closed_at` past the extract), and (b) the age-so-far of every missing incident (8.5–17.2 days as of the extract) is internally consistent with "still working it," not with a gap in export mechanics (there is no missing row for an incident that any comparable-severity peer closed in). This reading is inferred from internal consistency, not an independent source, so it is asserted with that caveat rather than treated as externally confirmed.
